faq.q: What is the gas constant R?
faq.a: The universal gas constant R = 8.314 J/(mol·K), used in the ideal gas law to relate pressure, volume, temperature, and moles.

faq.q: What are standard temperature and pressure (STP)?
faq.a: STP conditions are 0°C (273K) and 1 atm (101,325 Pa). At STP, one mole of ideal gas occupies 22.4 liters.

faq.q: When does the ideal gas law not apply?
faq.a: At high pressures or very low temperatures, real gases deviate from ideal behavior. The law works best for gases at moderate conditions.

faq.q: What are real-world applications of the ideal gas law?
faq.a: Applications include calculating balloon volumes, tire pressure, air density, refrigeration systems, and weather prediction models.

faq.q: How does the ideal gas law relate to air conditioning?
faq.a: Air conditioning works by compressing and expanding gas, which changes its temperature and pressure based on the ideal gas law.
